## Non-Destructive Control Statement

No UI was changed. No navigation was changed. No permissions were changed. No TXQuote, reporting, documents, scoped services, MGA UI, or end-user behavior was changed. No frontend reads were replaced. No records were seeded, repaired, quarantined, or mutated. No `master_general_agent_id` was assigned. No `master_group_id` was assigned. No Phase 4B was begun. This entire report is a read-only scan, dry-run calculation, and reconciliation result.

- **GLOBAL/PLATFORM** — not MGA-scoped by design; no action required
- **NOT PRESENT** — entity type contains zero records

**Key finding — 2 out-of-batch-scope BenefitCases identified:**
Records `8002` and `8846` are live operational BenefitCases that were not part of the remediation batch because their employer_group_id links resolve correctly (cd90 and cd91 respectively, both migrated to NBG). They have no `mga_migration_status` field. They are deterministically mappable to NBG via the EmployerGroup → MasterGroup → MGA chain. These are **Phase 4B backfill candidates** — they do not require new business approval (the parent chain is already approved) but do require Phase 4B final backfill execution to receive their `master_general_agent_id` and `master_group_id` stamps.

It carries no `mga_migration_status` field and was not in the remediation batch scope. It cannot receive MGA scope until its parent BenefitCase is resolved. No new quarantine action is required in the second dry-run (that would be a RE-09 function, which found zero eligible records; this record has no `mga_migration_status` field and thus was not in the `not_migrated` scan pool). This is documented for Phase 4B planning.
